field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: The separation of the polymers was achieved at chromatographic conditions that correspond to the critical point of PS and the size-exclusion mode of PMMA.
field_evidence.critical_component: The separation of the polymers was achieved at chromatographic conditions that correspond to the critical point of PS and the size-exclusion mode of PMMA.
field_evidence.column_name: The chromatographic separations were conducted on Macherey-Nagel Nucleosil C18 300-5 and 1000-7 with column sizes of 250 x 4 mm inner diameter.
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: Normal HPLC grade ACN and THF were used as the mobile phase.
field_evidence.mobile_phase_ratio: The critical conditions correspond to a mobile phase composition of THF–ACN of 48:52 v/v in agreement with ref.[[21]]
field_evidence.temperature_celsius: The critical diagram for using Nucleosil RP-18 columns at ambient temperature (22 8C).
field_evidence.flow_rate: The flow rate was 0.5 mL min-1.

conditions.physical_address: German Institute for Polymers, Schlossgartenstr. 6, 64289 Darmstadt, Germany
conditions.notes: PS is chromatographically invisible; PMMA elutes in SEC mode.
conditions.corresponding_author_name: Wolf Hiller; Pritish Sinha; Harald Pasch
